original text:
This Hell! government against itself: The state radical democracy Venezuela.
government against itself: The state radical democracy Venezuela.
Chavez gives this speech late 2012 where he's berating his government ministers it's his last major speech and part what he's trying lay out his legacy, and make clear what stands for. And that speech becomes perfectly clear stands for the commune, and the development alternative form production and radical democracy that stands against the existing state.
Political theorist George Ciccariello-Maher explores the political and economic history the Venezuelan commune, from its pre-Chavez roots popular uprising against the neoliberal order, the challenges sustaining direct democracy and socialist principles the face vast demographic changes, oil market fluctuations and capitalist resistance.
George Ciccariello-Maher author and Associate Professor Politics and Global Studies Drexel University Philadelphia.

original text:
May 2017 There will soon another quarterback entering the broadcast booth with the news that Fox Sports has hired Jay Cutler NFL game analyst. The move comes the wake CBS naming Tony Romo their top analyst, with Phil Simms heading "The NFL Today" studio show.
Cutler, 11-year veteran with the Denver Broncos and Chicago Bears, will part the No. broadcast team three-man booth alongside play-by-announcer Kevin Burkhardt and analyst Charles Davis. This will allow the 34-year-old get his feet wet the same way that Troy Aikman did when entered broadcasting 2002, working with Joe Buck and Cris Collinsworth.
Cutler, who holds almost every passing record Bears franchise history, was released the team March. was discussions with several teams, including the New York Jets, but opted join Fox. However, the former All-Pro has not ruled out the idea playing again.
don't know retirement the right word; don't feel that anyone ever really retires from the NFL," Cutler said. "You are either forced leave, you lose the desire what's required keep going. I'm between those situations this point life."
It's unclear Cutler was given clause his contract that would allow him return the field. But was, the three-man booth would also serve safety net for Fox mid-season injury forces team come calling for his services.

original text:
Can raspberry cordial kill stomach bugs contaminated water?
Have you ever used cordial prevent gastro bugs? Have your say the messageboard below.
For most us, cordial off the shopping list; avoid because it's full sugar, bad for our teeth and some its artificial colourings can make some kids hyperactive.
But raspberry cordial has some very loyal fans, who don't necessarily enjoy its sweet taste.
These people keep the pantry because they swear can prevent and possibly treat stomach bugs. And they may right, says microbiologist Heather Cavanagh, whose research found some raspberry juice and blackcurrant juice cordials can kill least different types nasty bacteria responsible for stomach bugs, including Coli and several strains salmonella.
Cavanagh first became interested the use raspberry cordial preventative and treatment stomach bugs when she heard that some farmers parts New South Wales and Victoria were adding raspberry cordial their livestock's drinking water treat diarrhoea.
She also began hear stories other people using raspberry cordial treat stomach bugs: heard anecdotal reports from far away Queensland and Western Australia, heard how people used treat themselves and their family. Not just their animals".
she decided see there was any science support these anecdotal reports.
the lab, Cavanagh tested juice made from number fresh berries including raspberries, blackberries, blackcurrants, cranberries, mulberries, loganberries and boysenberries well commercially available cordials made from these berries (these cordials were made with real berry juice, not apple juice and artificial colours sometimes the case).
These juices and cordials were then diluted down with water different strengths, the strongest was one part water five parts cordial and the weakest was 1:100. Then each type bacteria was mixed with each the berry drinks. The researchers found number the raspberry and blackcurrant juice cordials killed most the bacteria.
And while there has been some speculation that it's the sugar content cordials that kills bacteria, Cavanagh says there something unique raspberry and blackcurrant juices, which seems responsible.
Those cordials that had the best antibacterial properties contained least per cent raspberry blackcurrant juice, and worked when diluted down one part cordial parts water. Undiluted raspberry juice and blackcurrant juice worked just well the cordials.
But, Cavanagh says, there's hitch. While her research suggests there's some truth the red cordial story, there haven't been any clinical trials testing whether raspberry cordial can prevent treat stomach bugs humans animals.
"We know for certain that adding the right amount these cordials water will reduce the number bacteria the water," she says.
"[But] cannot say for certain that the cordial will definitely prevent stomach bugs people, and don't know yet will help treatment you are already feeling sick. Although again there some anecdotal evidence suggest that might."
And even red cordial does work, stomach bugs (gastroenteritis) are caused range things other than bacteria such viruses, parasites, and certain medications and raspberry cordial won't help these cases.
Also careful about drinking too much cordial (even diluted 1:10 ratio) you have vomiting diarrhoea. Drinks with high sugar content such undiluted soft drinks, sports drinks, juices strong cordials can promote fluid loss into the bowel and worsen dehydration, which risk for anyone with vomiting and diarrhoea.
Heather Cavanagh senior lecturer microbiology the School Biomedical Sciences Charles Sturt University. She spoke Claudine Ryan.

original text:
SECURITY MANAGEMENT software and door control hardware are your business's first line defense when comes loss prevention. Starr Telecom offers the Infinias access control system, which makes access control easy. Simply install one controller locally each door and connect the nearest Power over Ethernet switch with single Cat-5/6 cable.
Add doors anywhere your network goes, making easy and cost effective build system one door time. Combine the door controller with our access management software create highly scalable solution that can deployed within one building, across multiple locations around the world.
